title: deschampsia cespitosa subsp. mezensis
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. mezensis, also known as Deschampsia cespitosa, is a subspecies of a perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Portugal and Spain, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. mezensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional remedies, such as salves and poultices, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
